# Field experiments of success-breeds-success dynamics

**Authors:** Arnout van de Rijt, Soong Moon Kang, Michael Restivo, Akshay Patil  
**Year:** 2014  
**URL:** https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/24778230/  
**Evidence type:** Randomized field experiments

## Claim supported

Arbitrarily bestowed early success can cause subsequent success across domains.

## Key result

Early success improved subsequent rates of success compared with controls across funding, status, endorsement, and reputation systems.

## How it supports manufactured traction

This source strengthens the argument that digital systems can be moved by manipulated proxy signals. Use it as evidence for the mechanism, not as proof that every campaign succeeds.
